repair_candidate.summary: Repair #93897 so the legacy plugins.entries.openai-codex doctor migration preserves legacy fields when plugins.entries.openai already exists, reports merge vs rewrite accurately, and warns on true conflicts instead of silently dropping legacy values.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(doctor): preserve legacy openai-codex plugin entry fields
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## Summary
- Repair the legacy `plugins.entries.openai-codex` to `plugins.entries.openai` doctor migration when both entries exist.
- Preserve non-conflicting legacy fields instead of deleting them, report `Merged...` for merge paths, and surface true conflicts as warnings/manual-review change messages.
- Keep the focused #93897 contribution by @lsr911 as the canonical branch and carry forward @Nas01010101's issue/review findings.

## Credit
Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897 by @lsr911.
Issue and review context: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93891 by @Nas01010101.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- rerun real-behavior-proof for #93897 after repair
credit_notes: Preserve source PR credit for @lsr911 on https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93897.
Preserve issue and review attribution for @Nas01010101 from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/93891 and review comments on #93897.
Keep repair on the contributor branch because maintainer_can_modify=true; no replacement PR is needed unless branch repair becomes unsafe.

fix_actions.reason: Codex /review did not pass after 3 attempt(s): Merge is blocked. Security-sensitive issues look absent, and the supplied `pnpm check:changed` proof is sufficient for this changed surface, but the branch still deletes conflicting legacy plugin-entry values while only reporting their paths. That leaves the ClawSweeper review-bot finding on PR #93897 not fully addressed.
